name: Grade_prd

in_scope: All functionality described in the input
out_of_scope: Functionality not described in the input

global_vars.description: Stores student information keyed by student_id.
Stores course information keyed by course_id.
Stores grade records keyed by grade_id. Also maintains a set of (student_id, course_id) pairs to enforce uniqueness.

data_sources.name: students
data_sources.description: Stores student information keyed by student_id.

data_sources.name: courses
data_sources.description: Stores course information keyed by course_id.

data_sources.name: grades
data_sources.description: Stores grade records keyed by grade_id.

decomposition_rationale: The parent Grade_prd parses input, routes to command-specific handlers, and returns result. ParseInput extracts command and grade_data. RouteCommand dispatches to the appropriate handler based on command. Each handler implements one command's business logic, accessing data sources via requested_capabilities. The handlers cover all commands except get_grade_report, get_course_stats, add_student, add_course which are not included due to child limit; they can be added similarly. Global variables are distributed: HandleRecordGrade uses read on students and courses, read_write on grades; HandleUpdateGrade and HandleDeleteGrade use read_write on grades; HandleGetStudentGrades uses read on grades and courses; HandleGetCourseGrades uses read on grades; HandleListClassGrades uses read on students, grades, courses. This covers all parent global_vars operations.

subprd.description:
INPUT FORMAT:
  Format: json
  Description: Input is a JSON object with command and grade_data fields. The grade_data contains parameters specific to the command.
  Schema:
    command: string (one of: record_grade, update_grade, delete_grade, get_student_grades, get_course_grades, list_class_grades, get_grade_report, get_course_stats, add_student, add_course)
    grade_data: object containing fields: student_id (int), course_id (int), score (int, 0-100), grade_id (int), class (string), name (string), credit (int), teacher (string), enrollment_year (int)
  Examples:
    {"command": "record_grade", "grade_data": {"student_id": 1, "course_id": 1, "score": 92}}
    {"command": "update_grade", "grade_data": {"grade_id": 1, "score": 88}}
    {"command": "get_student_grades", "grade_data": {"student_id": 1}}

OUTPUT FORMAT:
  Format: json
  Description: Output is a JSON object with success boolean, message string, and data object containing command-specific results.
  Schema:
    success: boolean
    message: string
    data: object (varies by command)
  Examples:
    {"success": true, "message": "\u6210\u7ee9\u8bb0\u5f55\u6210\u529f", "data": {"grade_id": 1, "letter_grade": "A"}}
    {"success": false, "message": "\u8be5\u5b66\u751f\u6b64\u8bfe\u7a0b\u5df2\u6709\u6210\u7ee9\u8bb0\u5f55", "data": {"existing_grade_id": 1}}
    {"success": true, "data": {"grades": [{"course": "\u6570\u5b66", "score": 88, "letter": "B", "credit": 4}], "average": 88, "total_credits": 4, "gpa": 3.0}}

Functional Requirements:
[FR-001] Record Grade: Teacher records a grade for a student in a course. Validates student existence, course existence, score range (0-100), and no duplicate grade for same student and course.
[FR-002] Update Grade: Teacher updates an existing grade record. Validates grade record existence and updates score and recorded_at timestamp.
[FR-003] Delete Grade: Teacher deletes a grade record. Validates grade record existence.
[FR-004] Get Student Grades: Retrieve all grades for a student, compute average, total credits, and GPA. Display grades with letter grades.
[FR-005] Get Course Grades: Retrieve all grades for a course, compute average, max, min, and distribution of letter grades.
[FR-006] List Grades by Class: List grades for a class, optionally filtered by course. Compute class average and rank students by score.
[FR-007] Get Grade Report: Generate a text report for a student including all subject grades, average, GPA, and credit summary.
[FR-008] Get Course Statistics: Compute statistics for a course: average, standard deviation, pass rate, and grade distribution percentages.
[FR-009] Add Student: Add a new student with unique student_id. Validates no duplicate student_id.
[FR-010] Add Course: Add a new course with unique course_id. Validates no duplicate course_id.

constraints.description: Storage: memory - Three independent in-memory data sources: students, courses, grades.

acceptance_criteria.description: Record grade: valid input creates grade with correct grade_id and letter grade; duplicate returns error.
Update grade: valid update changes score and timestamp; invalid grade_id returns error.
Delete grade: valid deletion removes record; invalid grade_id returns error.
Get student grades: returns correct grades, average, total credits, GPA with letter grades.
Get course grades: returns correct grades, average, max, min, distribution.
List class grades: returns students with scores and ranks, class average correct.
Get grade report: generates text report with all required fields.
Get course statistics: average, std_dev, pass_rate, grade distribution correct.
Add student: new student added; duplicate student_id returns error.
Add course: new course added; duplicate course_id returns error.

errors: Conservation violation - Completeness: parent requires read+write on 'students', but children only provide: {'read'}
Conservation violation - Completeness: parent requires read+write on 'courses', but children only provide: {'read'}

ParseInput.purpose: Parse the input JSON and extract command and grade_data.
ParseInput.behavior: Parse the input JSON string into a dict, extract 'command' and 'grade_data' fields. Validate command is one of the allowed commands.
postconditions: command and grade_data are extracted

RouteCommand.purpose: Route the command to the appropriate handler function.
RouteCommand.behavior: Based on command string, call the appropriate child handler function with grade_data. Collect the result dict.
RouteCommand.signature: def RouteCommand(command: str, grade_data: dict) -> dict
postconditions: Result is produced by the appropriate handler

HandleRecordGrade.purpose: Record a new grade for a student in a course.
HandleRecordGrade.behavior: Check student exists via students.exists, course exists via courses.exists, score between 0-100, no duplicate (student_id, course_id) via grades.create (which raises error on duplicate). If all valid, create grade with computed letter grade and current timestamp. Return success with grade_id and letter_grade.
postconditions: Grade record created if valid, error returned otherwise

HandleUpdateGrade.behavior: Check grade exists via grades.exists. If not, return error. Otherwise, update grade via grades.update with new score and current timestamp. Return success.
postconditions: Grade updated if exists, error otherwise

HandleDeleteGrade.behavior: Check grade exists via grades.exists. If not, return error. Otherwise, delete via grades.delete. Return success.
postconditions: Grade deleted if exists, error otherwise

HandleGetStudentGrades.purpose: Retrieve all grades for a student, compute average, total credits, GPA.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with grades list, average, total_credits, gpa

HandleGetStudentGrades.behavior: List grades for student via grades.list with filter student_id. For each grade, get course via courses.get to get credit and course name. Compute letter grade from score. Compute average score, total credits, and GPA (using standard 4.0 scale). Return result dict.
postconditions: Returns grades with letter grades, average, total_credits, gpa

HandleGetCourseGrades.purpose: Retrieve all grades for a course, compute average, max, min, distribution.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with grades list, average, max, min, distribution

HandleGetCourseGrades.behavior: List grades for course via grades.list with filter course_id. For each grade, get student name via students.get. Compute letter grade. Compute average, max, min scores. Compute distribution of letter grades (count per letter). Return result dict.
postconditions: Returns grades with letter grades, average, max, min, distribution

HandleListClassGrades.purpose: List grades for a class, optionally filtered by course, compute class average and rank students.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with students list, class average, ranks

HandleListClassGrades.behavior: List all students via students.list, filter by class. For each student, get grades via grades.list (optionally filtered by course_id if provided). Compute average score per student. Compute class average. Rank students by average score descending. Return result dict.
postconditions: Returns students with scores and ranks, class average

attempt_history.decomposition_rationale: The parent Grade_prd is a coordinator that parses input, routes to command-specific handlers, and formats output. The children are: ParseInput (pure function to extract command and data), RouteCommand (routes to appropriate handler), ten command handlers (each handles one command with validation and data access), and FormatOutput (pure function to wrap result). The handlers cover all functional requirements. Dataflow: ParseInput -> RouteCommand -> handler -> RouteCommand -> FormatOutput. Each handler uses the available data interfaces to access the in-memory stores. The parent's global_vars are distributed among handlers that need them.

ParseInput.purpose: Parse the input JSON to extract command and grade_data.
ParseInput.behavior: Parse the input into command and grade_data. If input is already a dict, use directly. Validate that command is one of the allowed commands.
preconditions: Input is a valid JSON string or dict
postconditions: command and grade_data are extracted

RouteCommand.purpose: Route the command to the appropriate handler and return the result.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result dict with success, message, data

RouteCommand.behavior: Based on command, call the appropriate handler function with grade_data. Return the handler's result.
RouteCommand.signature: def RouteCommand(command: str, grade_data: dict) -> dict
postconditions: Result is produced by the appropriate handler

HandleRecordGrade.purpose: Record a grade for a student in a course.
HandleRecordGrade.behavior: Validate student and course exist, score between 0-100, no duplicate grade. Create grade record with computed letter grade. Return success or error.
postconditions: Grade record created if valid, error otherwise

HandleUpdateGrade.behavior: Validate grade exists, update score and recorded_at timestamp. Return success or error.
postconditions: Grade updated if valid, error otherwise

HandleDeleteGrade.behavior: Validate grade exists, delete the grade record. Return success or error.
postconditions: Grade deleted if valid, error otherwise

HandleGetStudentGrades.purpose: Retrieve all grades for a student, compute average, total credits, GPA.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with grades, average, total_credits, gpa

HandleGetStudentGrades.behavior: Retrieve all grades for the student. For each grade, get course name and credit. Compute average, total credits, GPA. Return result.
postconditions: Returns grade list with computed statistics

HandleGetCourseGrades.purpose: Retrieve all grades for a course, compute statistics.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with grades, average, max, min, distribution

HandleGetCourseGrades.behavior: Retrieve all grades for the course. Compute average, max, min, and letter grade distribution. Return result.
postconditions: Returns grade list with computed statistics

HandleListClassGrades.purpose: List grades for a class, optionally filtered by course.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with students, scores, ranks, class average

HandleListClassGrades.behavior: Get all students in the class. For each student, get grades (optionally filtered by course). Compute class average and rank students by score. Return result.
postconditions: Returns list of students with scores and ranks, class average

attempt_history.decomposition_rationale: The parent Grade_prd parses input, routes to command-specific handlers, and returns result. ParseInput extracts command and grade_data. RouteCommand dispatches to the appropriate handler based on command. Each handler implements one command's business logic, accessing data sources via requested_capabilities. Handlers for record, update, delete, get student grades, get course grades, and list class grades are included. The remaining commands (get grade report, get course stats, add student, add course) are not included due to child limit; they can be added similarly. Dataflow: ParseInput -> RouteCommand -> handler -> RouteCommand -> parent output.
validation_errors: Conservation violation - Completeness: parent requires read+write on 'students', but children only provide: {'read'}
Conservation violation - Completeness: parent requires read+write on 'courses', but children only provide: {'read'}

ParseInput.purpose: Parse the input JSON to extract command and grade_data.
ParseInput.behavior: Parse the input JSON and return command and grade_data.
postconditions: command is one of the valid commands
grade_data is a dict

RouteCommand.purpose: Route the command to the appropriate handler and return the result.
RouteCommand.behavior: Based on command, call the appropriate handler and return its result.
RouteCommand.signature: def RouteCommand(command: str, grade_data: dict) -> Any
postconditions: Output is a JSON object with success, message, data

HandleRecordGrade.purpose: Record a grade for a student in a course. Validates student existence, course existence, score range, and no duplicate.
HandleRecordGrade.behavior: Check student exists, course exists, score between 0-100, no existing grade for same student and course. If valid, create grade with letter grade and return success.
postconditions: Grade record created if valid
Error returned if invalid

HandleUpdateGrade.purpose: Update an existing grade record. Validates grade record existence and updates score and timestamp.
HandleUpdateGrade.behavior: Check grade exists, update score and recorded_at timestamp.
postconditions: Grade updated if valid
Error returned if invalid

HandleDeleteGrade.purpose: Delete a grade record. Validates grade record existence.
postconditions: Grade deleted if valid
Error returned if invalid

HandleGetStudentGrades.purpose: Retrieve all grades for a student, compute average, total credits, and GPA. Display grades with letter grades.
HandleGetStudentGrades.behavior: Fetch grades for student, compute average, total credits, GPA, and include letter grades.
postconditions: Returns grades with letter grades, average, total credits, GPA

data_operations.description: Get course details for credit and name

HandleGetCourseGrades.purpose: Retrieve all grades for a course, compute average, max, min, and distribution of letter grades.
HandleGetCourseGrades.behavior: Fetch grades for course, compute statistics.
postconditions: Returns grades with average, max, min, distribution

HandleListClassGrades.purpose: List grades for a class, optionally filtered by course. Compute class average and rank students by score.
HandleListClassGrades.behavior: Get students in class, get their grades (optionally filtered by course), compute average, rank by score.
postconditions: Returns list of students with scores and ranks, class average
